search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae rashes assessment history morphology distribution red flags
site:nice.org.uk rash assessment morphology mucosal blistering
candidate_sources_rejected: search snippets naming adult rash differentials
rejection_reasons: No exact general-adult rash guideline section was established.
Treatment pages did not support documentation-only fields.
Image tools could imply an autonomous diagnosis.
population_applicability: Direct only for children with rash; the unrestricted GP rash workflow also includes adults, pregnancy, and other contexts that remain unsupported.
setting_applicability: Direct for DHA-licensed telehealth pediatric rash history and red-flag documentation; only partial for in-person general practice.
UAE_applicability: Verified for Dubai pediatric telehealth only, not for unrestricted UAE adult or in-person rash care.
recency_verification: Current DHA Issue 2 PDF, due for revision 2029-02-21, was reviewed on 2026-07-13.
superseded_check: No newer official DHA issue was identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Constrains the exact UAE evidence to pediatric telehealth.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines pediatric rash use in DHA-licensed telehealth and excludes emergency and controlled-medication scenarios.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports rash, itching, and fever grouping without diagnosing a cause.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Organises pediatric rashes by fever, itching, and neither fever nor itching without proving a diagnosis for an individual child.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports distribution, morphology, evolution, symptoms, exposures, medicines, travel, allergy, and visual assessment fields for children.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Specifies visual assessment plus duration, evolution, distribution, symptoms, family history, recurrence, exposures, medication, impact, travel, allergy, and pet history.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports fever, systemic symptoms, rapid or persistent concern, immunocompromise, and diagnostic uncertainty as clinician prompts only.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists emergency and face-to-face review criteria, including confusion, stiff neck, photophobia, persistent or recurrent rash, fever, immunocompromise, and diagnostic uncertainty.

unresolved_source_gaps: Adult, pregnancy-associated, purpuric, medicine-associated, blistering, infectious, and lesion-specific rash pathways remain incompletely sourced.
Skin swab, blood tests, skin-care advice, trigger avoidance, fixed follow-up timing, and all diagnostic conclusions remain unsupported.
